So, Mike Vance built a, mostly with Fortune 1000s, not with entrepreneurs, but a terrific career as a speaker, consultant. He wouldn't use the term coach, but client groups, 10, 20, 30 CEOs and executives in a group paying $4,000 or $5,000 a month in order to get together in what he would call creative brainstorming sessions, what we would call a mastermind session or a coaching group. So Mike very much ran our kind of business without ever seeing what we're doing and without really understanding what we're doing. Mike's entire, entire career is based on having worked for Walt Disney for three years. That's it. And if you take that away, you've got no Vance career. You've got no story. But the connection to Disney, powerful. Smart enough never to try to walk away from it. So for 30 years, Mike's been telling Walt Disney stories. Mike's had himself hooked to Disney at the hip. Smart enough. Never to try and separate. Ego not in a way. Never want to make this about Mike Vance. Make this about Mike Vance as the closest you can get to Walt. Helps Walt's con. Can't get to Walt unless you're going to do the seance. By the way, did you think more about that? Are you going to do it? Yeah, we're doing it. Okay. We're going to give it his boot camp doing a seance. So who are you going to talk to? Perfect. So people at his boot camp are going to talk to the dead Andrew Carnegie. We have no shame. You should learn the skill from him and then you can do Napoleon Hill. Who are those people you like who channel the Martian? What's their stick? They're channeling somebody, right? Yes. They're not doing it. They actually believe it. Well, that's irrelevant, isn't it? But I mean, it's a great stick. Walt Vance is nearly channeling Disney. He just doesn't use that language, but that's Vance and that's what you're buying. You're buying Disney and mostly you're buying Disney stories. Now they have a few methodologies that came from Disney. So their storyboard methodology is an organizational planning kind of marketing thing. The five sensing thing came from Disney. But Vance attached himself to that. I mean, not, I wouldn't call it a mountain from a molehill, but a really big mountain from kind of a small mountain. I mean, somewhat embellishing the extent of the involvement and connection and all of that. So by strategy of attachment, the guy's a fascinating person who could command a lot of money from clients. Without the attachment, he's got no career at all. Dale Carnegie, the franchisees, are all connected to Dale. Without Dale, Foster Hubbard, who we had a relationship for five years, we created a business for Foster. So Foster's a speaker. I saw Foster speak in a chiropractic event where I was speaking. And so he was on before me. So I went not really because I cared about seeing him because I wanted to make sure I wasn't going to do anything stupid. He was the best speaker I've ever seen, period, to this day. It's disease now. Nobody's a better speaker. So worst platform sales person I ever saw in my life. I mean, you couldn't be worse. Pitiful. Whole body language, physiology changed when it came time to talk about product. I mean, he like shrunk. And here's this whole pitch. Some of you want tapes and there's tapes, there's a suitcase and put whatever you think I should have in the suitcase and take the tapes. This is pitch.
